task_completion.description: Verify the browser is open to the correct Jitsi meeting URL.
Verify both required participants have joined the meeting.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Both 'FitnessCoach' and 'ClassHelper' are visible in the meeting interface (e.g., on video tiles or in the participants list).

task_completion.description: Verify the participants panel is open.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The participants panel is visible, typically on the right side of the Jitsi interface, listing the people in the meeting.

task_completion.description: Verify interaction with the participant context menu.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The context menu (often represented by three dots) next to 'ClassHelper' in the participants panel is open.

task_completion.description: Verify the action to grant moderator rights is being executed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Grant moderator' (or similar) option is visible and being selected or clicked in the context menu for 'ClassHelper'.

task_completion.description: Verify 'ClassHelper' has been successfully granted moderator rights.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the participants panel, 'ClassHelper' has a moderator indicator (such as a star icon or 'Moderator' label) next to their name.

task_completion.description: Verify the final screenshot was saved to the correct path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Evidence that a screenshot was saved to '/home/ga/moderator_granted.png' (e.g., a terminal command like 'scrot' or 'import', a save dialog, or an image viewer displaying the file with this path).

integrity.description: Verify a real web browser was used to access the meeting.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show a genuine web browser interface (like Firefox) rendering the Jitsi Meet application.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the Jitsi GUI to grant permissions.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show the Jitsi interface menus or participant panel being used, rather than terminal commands or edited HTML to fake the state.

integrity.description: Verify the final screenshot is a genuine capture of the application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The image looks like a real screenshot of the Jitsi interface without signs of image manipulation, text pasting, or mockups.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The meeting URL is http://localhost:8080/virtual-fitness-class. The participants must be named 'FitnessCoach' (the original moderator) and 'ClassHelper'. The expected final state is that 'ClassHelper' has been granted moderator rights, indicated by a star icon or 'Moderator' label next to their name in the Jitsi participants panel. The expected screenshot path is /home/ga/moderator_granted.png.
